Title: Characterization of the Wheat Heat Shock Factor TaHsfA2e-5D Conferring Heat and Drought Tolerance in Arabidopsis

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: A 2027 bp 5′-flanking region upstream of the start codon was cloned, and promoter analysis revealed multiple stress-responsive cis-elements, including drought-responsive MBS sites, dehydration-responsive DRE, low-temperature-responsive LTR, MeJA-responsive TGACG motifs, and ABA-responsive ABRE elements.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. TaHsfA2e-5D is a wheat HsfA2-type transcription factor located on chromosome 5D, with a conserved DBD, HR-A/B domains, NLS, and a C-terminal AHA activation motif.
2. It is constitutively expressed in various wheat tissues and is strongly induced by heat, drought (PEG6000), salt, cold, and multiple phytohormones, consistent with the presence of stress-related cis-elements in its promoter.
3. TaHsfA2e-5D protein localizes to the nucleus and exhibits transcriptional activation activity in yeast, dependent on its C-terminal AHA motif.
4. Ectopic expression of TaHsfA2e-5D in yeast significantly improves thermotolerance, and overexpression in Arabidopsis enhances tolerance to both heat and drought stresses and reduces water loss.
5. In TaHsfA2e-5D-overexpressing Arabidopsis, several Hsp genes and other stress-related genes including GolS1 and APX2 show elevated transcript levels, indicating that TaHsfA2e-5D enhances stress tolerance at least partly by activating stress-responsive genes.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq data of TAM107 wheat seedlings under normal conditions and 6 h heat stress were mined to identify TaHsfA2e-5D as a strongly heat-induced Hsf gene (published dataset [25]).
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Wheat heat shock factor that functions as a nuclear transcriptional activator and positively regulates plant responses to heat and drought stresses, improving thermotolerance in yeast and enhancing heat and drought tolerance in Arabidopsis when overexpressed.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Acts as a nuclear transcriptional activator via its C-terminal AHA activation domain; GAL4-based yeast assays show that full-length TaHsfA2e-5D, but not a C-terminal 48-amino-acid deletion mutant lacking the AHA motif, activates reporter gene expression, indicating that the AHA region is required for transactivation. Overexpression enhances transcript levels of multiple Hsp and other stress-responsive genes, consistent with transcriptional activation of stress-response pathways.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in the heat shock and broader abiotic stress-response network by up-regulating Hsp genes and other stress-related genes (e.g., APX2, GolS1), thereby enhancing cellular protection mechanisms against heat and drought stresses.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: TaHsfA2e-5D -->(activation)--> Hsp17.7-CII, Hsp18.1-CI, Hsp22-ER, Hsp25.3-P, Hsp26.5-P, Hsp70t-2, GolS1, APX2 (Arabidopsis stress-responsive genes whose transcript levels are significantly higher in TaHsfA2e-5D overexpression lines than in wild type).
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Heat shock transcription factor of class A2 (HsfA2-type), containing a conserved N-terminal DNA-binding domain (DBD) and HR-A/B oligomerization domain typical of Hsfs, as well as a nuclear localization signal (NLS) and a C-terminal AHA transcriptional activation motif.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus-localized protein, as shown by TaHsfA2e-5D-GFP fusion fluorescence restricted to the nucleus in tobacco leaf epidermal cells compared with ubiquitous GFP signal of free GFP.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Constitutively expressed in multiple wheat tissues at the booting stage, with highest transcript levels in roots, stamens, and pistils, intermediate levels in glume and arista, and lowest levels in leaf, young spike, and stem. Strongly induced in wheat seedlings by high temperature (40 °C), low temperature (4 °C), PEG6000-induced dehydration, NaCl, MeJA, and ABA, and weakly but detectably induced by H2O2 and SA.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heat stress of 40 °C (wheat seedlings, yeast liquid culture), 45 °C for 2 h (Arabidopsis seedlings on plates), and 40/32 °C day/night for 7 days (Arabidopsis in soil); drought stress imposed by PEG6000 treatment in wheat seedlings and withholding water for 14 days in Arabidopsis; additional treatments for expression analysis included 4 °C cold, 20% PEG6000, 0.2 M NaCl, 0.1 mM MeJA, 0.2 mM ABA, 10 mM H2O2, and 0.1 mM SA.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress tolerance (heat and drought) regulated by wheat heat shock factors; functional characterization of TaHsfA2e-5D and its role in transcriptional control of stress-responsive genes.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: abiotic stress tolerance (heat stress tolerance, drought tolerance)
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In drought assays, survival of wild-type Arabidopsis was about 25%, whereas TaHsfA2e-5D overexpression lines L2, L3, and L4 showed more than 70% survival after 14 days without water followed by 2 days recovery, and exhibited significantly lower water loss rates. In yeast thermotolerance assays at 40–50 °C, pYES2-TaHsfA2e-5D transformants had significantly higher survival and higher OD600 values after 9 h of heat stress than empty-vector controls.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Overexpression of TaHsfA2e-5D in Arabidopsis did not cause obvious morphological changes under normal growth conditions compared with wild type.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Wheat cultivar TAM107 seedlings for cloning and expression analysis; Chinese Spring nulli-tetrasomic wheat lines for chromosomal location; tobacco (Nicotiana benthamiana) leaf epidermal cells for transient TaHsfA2e-5D-GFP localization; Arabidopsis thaliana Columbia-0 wild type and TaHsfA2e-5D overexpression lines (L1–L4); Saccharomyces cerevisiae strains AH109 (for GAL4 assays) and INVSc1 (for thermotolerance assays) transformed with TaHsfA2e-5D constructs.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: PCR-based cloning from genomic DNA and cDNA; RT-qPCR for tissue-specific and stress-induced expression; in silico promoter cis-element analysis (PlantCARE); subcellular localization using 35S:TaHsfA2e-5D-GFP transient expression and confocal microscopy; GAL4-based yeast transcriptional activation assay on SD/-Trp and SD/-Trp-His media; yeast thermotolerance assays with survival plating and growth curves under 40–50 °C; Arabidopsis transformation via floral dip, selection of single-copy homozygous lines, and heat and drought stress phenotyping; RT-qPCR of stress-related genes in Arabidopsis.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous overexpression in yeast (pYES2-TaHsfA2e-5D) and Arabidopsis thaliana (pSuper1300-TaHsfA2e-5D) followed by heat and drought stress assays; subcellular localization of TaHsfA2e-5D-GFP in tobacco epidermal cells; GAL4-based transcriptional activation assay in yeast using full-length and C-terminally truncated TaHsfA2e-5D; RT-qPCR analysis of downstream stress-responsive genes in transgenic Arabidopsis.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified from published transcriptome (RNA-seq) data of TAM107 wheat seedlings showing strong heat-induced up-regulation of a heat shock factor gene; genomic and coding sequences amplified from TAM107 DNA and cDNA by PCR with gene-specific primers; chromosomal location determined by PCR using chromosome-specific primers and Chinese Spring nulli-tetrasomic lines, placing TaHsfA2e-5D on chromosome 5D.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Provides a promising candidate gene for molecular breeding and genetic engineering of wheat and other crops to enhance tolerance to terminal heat and drought stresses by manipulating a stress-responsive heat shock transcription factor.
